# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.
# Category: container_security

# Use an existing docker image as a parent image 
FROM node:14 AS builder  
WORKDIR /app   
    
RUN apt-get update && \
	apt-get install -y build-essential git curl libpcre3-dev zlib1g-dev unzip make python software-properties-common # Install necessary tools 
     
# Copy package.json and yarn.lock from the current directory to /app  
COPY . ./   
    
RUN npm install -d --silent && \      
	npm cache verify &&\       
	# Cleanup installed packages by deleting node_modules in your project dir 
	rm -rf package-lock.json $(find . -name "node_modules")  
# Install app dependencies   
RUN npm install     # Add more if necessary (like 'yarn add <package>')     		         			        	 	   	     									      	       								       							 ​                           	                                                  → COPY --from=builder /app/dist . && \           RUN yarn build:prod  
# Expose port to access the app from outside.     # For production, use a reverse proxy like nginx or another service instead of exposing on PORT   2019-  SHELL [ "/bin/bash" ]     	      		         	  RUN echo "Starting Node server..." && node ./dist/server.js
